channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Multi-level context Modeling for consistent expert selection in Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: arXiv:2607.16427v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) enables efficient scaling of Transformer models by routing tokens to a small subset of experts. However, existing routers typically condition expert selection on shallow or isolated token representations, which often produce u...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, transformer, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RIMS: Preference Optimization via Smoothed Multi-pair Aggregation for Small-Scale LLM Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: arXiv:2607.16431v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Small-scale language models (SLMs) are attractive for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) in resource-constrained settings, but their limited capacity makes them highly sensitive to noisy or spurious retrieved evidence. Existing preference-based meth...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, chain-of-thought, arxiv, augmented, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Committed Before Reasoning: Behavioral Reproduction and Preliminary Activation-Level Evidence of Answer Pre-Commitment in an Open-Weight LLM
item.description: arXiv:2607.16451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chat models sometimes commit to an answer and then produce reasoning that justifies it rather than deriving it -- even when the answer contradicts a task premise. We study a minimal probe: "I want to wash my car. The car wash is 100 meters away. Shoul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, product, context, study, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Encoding EEG Signals to Examine Human-Like Next-Word Prediction Behaviour in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.16549v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models (LMs) are trained to excel at predicting the next word in the sequence given prior context, and humans also share this predictability in reading comprehension. Neuroscience research reveals that next-word predictability influences brai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, analysis, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NOWJ@COLIEE 2026: Adaptive Pipelines for Legal Retrieval and Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2607.16603v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents the methodologies and results of the NOWJ team's participation across all five tasks of the COLIEE 2026 competition. For Task 1 (Legal Case Retrieval), we propose a four-stage pipeline comprising candidate filtering, dense retrieva...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, chain-of-thought, model, reasoning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Memory to Skills: Evidence-Grounded Co-Evolution Governance for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.16621v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing memory systems for long-horizon LLM agents often retrieve prior traces as passive context rather than converting them into executable capabilities. In this paper, we propose MSCE, a training-free Memory--Skill Co-Evolution framework that orga...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, arxiv, paper, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenLanguageModel: Readable and Composable Small-Language-Model Pretraining for Education and Research
item.description: arXiv:2607.16669v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: OpenLanguageModel (OLM) is an open-source PyTorch library for building and pretraining small language models while keeping their machinery visible. In OLM, model code reads like the architecture: components are ordinary modules, while Block, Residual,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, arxiv, library, research, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SpecLA: Efficient Speculative Decoding for Linear-Attention Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.16673v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Linear-attention models replace the growing KV cache with recurrent states, but autoregressive decoding still reads, updates, and writes these states one token at a time. Speculative decoding can reduce this cost by verifying several draft tokens in o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer, paper, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Are Arithmetic Heuristic Neurons Form-Invariant? A Mechanistic Analysis of Symbols, Text, and Code in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.16693v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models often succeed on one formulation of a problem while failing on an equivalent formulation. Whether these failures arise from distinct internal circuits or different activation states of a shared circuit remains unknown. Recent mec...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, large language model, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Though Language Models Err While They Strive: Conformal Prediction for Self-Correcting Scientific Generation
item.description: arXiv:2607.16704v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models frequently violate fundamental scientific principles when generating technical content, undermining their reliability in scientific applications. We introduce Scientific Feasibility Control SFC, a graph-structured conformal predi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, reasoning, multimodal, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Rater State Bias in RLHF Preference Data: An Audit Framework
item.description: arXiv:2607.16195v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We identify a structured confound in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF). Pairwise preference labels are intended to reflect the compared outputs, but they may also reflect the rater's state during annotation. Under sustained stressful o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, RLHF, ICL, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Design and Validation of a Lightweight 1D CNN for Affective Touch Classification in Soft Plush Companions
item.description: arXiv:2607.16196v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Soft, sensorized companions offer a physically safe and emotionally intuitive interface for socially assistive technologies, yet their deformability and multichannel tactile sensing complicate the robust interpretation of human affect. This study pres...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, study, arxiv, embedding, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Some Large Language Models Exhibit Consistent Risk Attitudes
item.description: arXiv:2607.16197v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As artificial intelligence systems are deployed in open-ended, high-stakes settings, a critical dimension remains unmeasured: how perceived risk is translated into action. We test whether large language models (LLMs) exhibit systematic and consistent ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, arxiv, large language model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PlanFlip: Attacking Multi-Agent LLM Systems via Planning-Phase Prompt Injection
item.description: arXiv:2607.16199v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent LLM systems increasingly rely on a Planner to decompose goals into sub-task sequences that downstream Executor and Critic agents execute and audit. We identify the planning phase as a critical attack surface: a single injection into the Pl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, tool, GPT, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Deterministic Replay for AI Agent Systems
item.description: arXiv:2607.16200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agent systems that couple large language models (LLMs) with external tools and APIs are inherently non-deterministic: LLM sampling variance, external API state, CDN infrastructure headers, and execution-environment noise collectively prevent any pr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, platform, model, tool, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Generative Ontology Induction: Domain-Agnostic Schema Discovery from Document Corpora Using Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.16201v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Ontology engineering remains a critical bottleneck in knowledge-intensive AI systems. Existing automated approaches either depend on predefined schemas, operate within narrow domains, or produce unstructured outputs unsuitable for downstream pipelines...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> template, arxiv, example, prompt, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Democratizing AI with Small Language Models: Structured Benchmarking and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Local Deployment
item.description: arXiv:2607.16202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI democratization is not primarily a question of matching frontier-scale generality; it is a question of whether capable models can be selected, audited, and specialized under hardware and governance constraints that ordinary institutions can actuall...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, fine-tuning, arxiv, example, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Masked Diffusion Language Models are Strong and Steerable Text-Based World Models for Agentic RL
item.description: arXiv:2607.16204v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent growth in reinforcement learning (RL) has surfaced a need for diverse, specialized training environments. Hand-curated environments with fixed task and reward difficulties become ineffective signals as model performance improves, and sparse rew...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, fine-tuning, context, tool, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DocOCR-Eval: A Correction-Based Framework for OCR Tool Selection Without Ground Truth
item.description: arXiv:2607.16203v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Document parsing is a foundational step for document understanding tasks such as visual question answering and key information extraction, as it transforms unstructured scanned images into structured representations by extracting textual, visual, and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, image, tool, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Fully-sensorized smart-eyewear platform for on-device Machine Learning
item.description: arXiv:2607.16222v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents ARGO, a smart eyewear platform designed to bridge ergonomic comfort, high computational throughput, and energy efficiency. Unlike cloud-dependent solutions, ARGO leverages the STM32N6 microcontroller and its integrated Neural Proce...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, multimodal, arxiv, paper, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Operator-Aware Mixed-Precision Tolerance Calibration for Tensor Kernels
item.description: arXiv:2607.16228v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most tensor-kernel correctness tests go through a fixed-shape all close-style check with hand-picked absolute and relative tolerances. The thresholds are copied across the corpus and rarely revisited. We mine the element-wise error distribution of eve...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, attention, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Orthogonal Gradient Constraints Shape Noisy-Label Memorization Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2607.16231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern neural networks can fit corrupted training labels, making noisy-label learning a useful setting for studying memorization-driven overfitting. Most regularization methods modify the objective, architecture, or data distribution; here we instead ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, study, vector, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Weights to Words: Expressing and Editing Preference Model Inferences in Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2607.16232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The growing use of statistical learning algorithms to infer human preferences from high-dimensional choice data runs up against a fundamental challenge: choice alternatives typically differ in many ways simultaneously, so it is generally unclear which...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vector, arxiv, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Token-Level Cross-Modal Transformer with Contrastive Multi-Task Learning for Breast Cancer Subtype Classification and Survival Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2607.16233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Integrating heterogeneous genomic and clinical modalities for joint cancer subtype classification and survival prediction remains a key challenge in precision oncology. Existing approaches suffer from three limitations: (1) they treat each modality as...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, arxiv, transformer, RAG, cross-modal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt, context window, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, RAG, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vector, reasoning, framework, knowledge base | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: It Takes 8 Tokens: Weak-to-Strong Off-Policy RL via Auxiliary Branches
item.description: arXiv:2607.16205v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has emerged as a standard approach for enhancing reasoning in large language models, which typically optimizes the policy by contrasting multiple self generated rollouts. However, we identify a critical s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, arxiv, paper, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLM Unlearning for Cyber Defense: A Survey on Methods, Challenges, and Emerging Threats
item.description: arXiv:2607.16227v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are increasingly deployed in security-critical systems across healthcare, finance, education, and decision support, yet their inability to forget creates serious cybersecurity, privacy, and safety risks. Sensitive personal information, copyrighte...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompting, LLM, arxiv, prompt, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

This paper addresses constrained multi-objective optimization (MOO) with an application...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, paper, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.
item.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - A practical Claude Code guide with clear mental models and copy-paste examples — setup, prompt engineering, slash commands, skills, hooks, subagents, agent teams, and MCP servers. Beginner path to power-user depth. Featured in Awesome Claude Code.

item.title: A Survey on GNN-based Link Prediction: Techniques, Applications, and Challenges
item.description: arXiv:2607.16198v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have emerged as the leading paradigm for link prediction, enabling the inference of missing connections and the anticipation of potential future links. However, existing reviews lack systematic exploration specifically tar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HantaWatch: Federated Learning for Hantavirus Genomic Surveillance
item.description: arXiv:2607.16234v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hantavirus genomic surveillance is limited by the distribution of sequence data, non-IID source heterogeneity, and constrained expert-review capacity. We propose HantaWatch, a federated learning framework that enables laboratories and surveillance sit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: WrenAI - GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.
item.description: GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
